user_query:
I'm prepping our forum's monthly alt-genre "Sampler" and want it to be data-driven. Using the two local inputs provided in input/submissions.csv and input/genre_weights.json, please select and order a short playlist and generate a concise write-up I can post.

Requirements:
1) Compute a weighted score for each track as: weighted_score = upvotes * genre_weight[microgenre], defaulting to 1.0 for any microgenre not present in the weights file.
2) Choose a playlist that satisfies all of the following constraints:
   - Total duration between 23 and 26 minutes inclusive (1380–1560 seconds).
   - Track count between 5 and 8 inclusive.
   - No artist appears more than once.
   - Include at least 4 distinct microgenres.
   - Include tracks from at least 3 different countries.
   - At most 2 tracks from any single microgenre.
3) Order the chosen playlist as follows:
   - Position 1 must be the selected track with the highest weighted_score among those with duration_sec <= 180; if none of the selected tracks are <= 180s, position 1 is simply the highest weighted_score track.
   - Positions 2..N in descending weighted_score; break ties by shorter duration first, then by id lexicographically.
4) Write a structured machine-readable file to output/playlist.json with:
   - tracks: an array of objects including id, position (1-based), artist, title, microgenre, country, duration_sec, upvotes, weighted_score.
   - summary: an object with total_duration_sec, track_count, distinct_microgenres, distinct_countries, total_weighted_score.
5) Compose a short, forum-ready summary at output/sampler_summary.md that includes:
   - A brief overview of the approach and how weighted_score was computed.
   - A list of the selected tracks with their positions and weighted_score.
   - A "Constraints Check" section that explicitly reports: total duration (sec and mm:ss), track count, number of distinct microgenres, number of distinct countries, whether any artist repeats, and whether the microgenre-per-genre limit was respected, each with pass/fail labels.
   - A brief "Notes on Exclusions" section naming at least three higher-scoring tracks that were not selected and explaining the main reason each was left out (e.g., artist duplicate, microgenre cap, duration feasibility).

Please ensure both output files are created and consistent with each other. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
